findings.evidence: CommutationRelabeling.lean:166-169; Diagram.lean:698-705 provides the explicit application lemmas.
findings.fix: Replace `rfl` with `simp [ColumnsNoninterleaving]`, which discharges it via the existing `relabelColumns_O_apply`/`relabelColumns_X_apply` simp lemmas (Diagram.lean:700-705).
findings.issue: `columnsNoninterleaving_relabelColumns` is proven by `rfl`, resting on defeq of `(G.relabelColumns κ).O a` with `G.O (κ.symm a)` across the GridDiagram field projection and the Equiv coercion. This is the exact undocumented-defeq-across-wrappers pattern; the swap analogue `columnsNoninterleaving_swapColumns` (line 195) instead uses `simp`, so this is inconsistent and more brittle.

findings.fix: Replace `rfl` with `simp [RowsNoninterleaving]` so it goes through `OColumnOfRow_relabelRows`/`XColumnOfRow_relabelRows`.
findings.issue: `rowsNoninterleaving_relabelRows` is proven by `rfl`, relying on defeq through `OColumnOfRow`→`columnOfRow`→`.toPerm.symm`→`relabelRows`'s invFun. The PR itself supplies `OColumnOfRow_relabelRows`/`XColumnOfRow_relabelRows` as the robust rewrite lemmas, yet they go unused here.

summary: Proofs are largely robust, but two Noninterleaving relabel lemmas are closed by bare `rfl`, relying on undocumented definitional equality through the GridDiagram/GridState wrappers and the DFunLike coercion. Explicit @[simp] application lemmas already exist and the swap analogues use them, so route these through `simp` for stability.
